verbGlossary.GOVERNED BY: {source} is regulated by the requirements of {target} or standard (e.g., building code edition governed by IBC, fire sprinkler governed by NFPA standards)
verbGlossary.CERTIFIED UNDER: {source} sustainability attribute is validated through the target certification program (e.g., LEED certification certified under LEED program, net zero target certified under resilience and net zero standards)
verbGlossary.CLASSIFIED BY: {source} is categorized using {target} system's taxonomy (e.g., delivery method classified by delivery method classification, structural system classified by UniFormat)
verbGlossary.DEFINED BY: {source} building attribute's allowable values are defined by {target} code (e.g., occupancy group defined by IBC occupancy classifications, construction type defined by IBC Type I-V)
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED TO: {source} performance metric is compared against {target} published norms (e.g., cost per SF and cost growth benchmarked to AACE cost engineering standards)
verbGlossary.REPORTED UNDER: {source} safety metric is formally recorded using {target}keeping classification framework
verbGlossary.CATEGORIZED BY: {source} safety metric incidents are classified using the target hazard or injury taxonomy (e.g., safety incidents categorized by OSHA hazard categories and ANSI injury {target}s)
verbGlossary.SCOPED BY: {source} identity attribute determines which sections of {target} system apply (e.g., project type scopes applicable MasterFormat divisions)
verbGlossary.STRUCTURES: {source} sector attribute determines the organization of {target} breakdown or code structure (e.g., sector structures the WBS code hierarchy for infrastructure vs. vertical construction)
